name: docs-creator
description: Create and organize public documentation pages using Fumadocs. Use when building new documentation pages or organizing existing ones.
capabilities: Create MDX documentation pages with proper frontmatter structure
Generate meta.json files for controlling sidebar navigation and folder organization
Configure page metadata including titles, descriptions, and Lucide icons
Organize nested documentation hierarchies with automatic URL routing
Reference Fumadocs UI components like Callout, Tabs, and Steps in MDX content
author: AayushBaniya2006
securitySummary: This skill is safe for publication. All 47 static findings are false positives from the analyzer misinterpreting Markdown syntax as code. The skill contains only documentation and reference materials with no executable code, network requests, or file system operations.
valueStatement: Building technical documentation requires consistent structure and routing. This skill helps you create well-organized MDX documentation pages with Fumadocs, handling frontmatter, navigation metadata, and icon integration automatically.
limitations: Requires Fumadocs to be already installed and configured in the project
Only works with the specific file structure pattern in src/content/docs/
Cannot modify Fumadocs configuration or customize rendering behavior
Does not validate that referenced Lucide icon names exist

useCases.title: API Documentation Structure
useCases.description: Create a complete API reference section with endpoint documentation, authentication guides, and code examples organized in a logical hierarchy.

useCases.title: Product Documentation
useCases.description: Build user-facing help documentation with getting started guides, feature walkthroughs, and troubleshooting sections with proper navigation.

useCases.title: Internal Knowledge Base
useCases.description: Organize team documentation including onboarding guides, architecture decisions, and development workflows in a searchable structure.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic Documentation Page
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a getting started page at src/content/docs/getting-started.mdx with title Getting Started and description Quick start guide for new users
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new documentation section with a simple introductory page

promptTemplates.title: Build API Reference Section
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an API folder in docs with a meta.json that organizes pages for authentication, endpoints, and webhooks with the Webhook icon
promptTemplates.scenario: Structuring comprehensive API documentation with multiple related pages

promptTemplates.title: Add Nested Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a guides/advanced folder with meta.json and add pages for caching, performance, and security with appropriate icons
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a multi-level documentation hierarchy for complex topics

promptTemplates.title: Organize Existing Pages
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the meta.json in src/content/docs to reorder pages putting introduction first, then installation, configuration, and add a separator before advanced topics
promptTemplates.scenario: Reorganizing documentation navigation after adding multiple pages

bestPractices: Always include title and description in MDX frontmatter for SEO and search functionality
Use descriptive Lucide icon names that match the content topic to improve visual navigation
Organize related pages into folders with meta.json to maintain clear documentation hierarchy
antiPatterns: Creating pages without frontmatter will result in missing metadata and poor search indexing
Using non-existent Lucide icon names will cause rendering errors in the sidebar
Placing pages outside src/content/docs/ will break the Fumadocs routing system

faq.question: What file format should documentation pages use?
faq.answer: Use MDX format with .mdx extension. MDX allows you to combine Markdown with React components from Fumadocs UI.

faq.question: How do I control the order of pages in the sidebar?
faq.answer: Create a meta.json file in the folder and list page filenames in the desired order in the pages array.

faq.question: Can I use custom icons for documentation sections?
faq.answer: You can use any Lucide icon name. Reference the Lucide icon library at lucide.dev to find available icon names.

faq.question: Where should I place documentation images and assets?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on page structure and metadata. Asset placement depends on your Fumadocs configuration, typically in a public or static folder.

faq.question: How do nested folders affect the URL structure?
faq.answer: Folders create URL segments automatically. For example, api/auth.mdx becomes /docs/api/auth in the browser.

faq.question: Does this skill modify existing Fumadocs configuration?
faq.answer: No, this skill only creates content pages and navigation metadata. It assumes Fumadocs is already installed and configured in your project.
